nodes.name: Cron — 3x daily (MSK)
nodes.notes: Fires three times per day aligned to the Telegram publish slots (09:00 / 12:00 / 18:00 MSK, stored as UTC in cron).

nodes.name: Node 1 — Check editorial calendar
notes: Endpoint: GET /api/v1/content/pending?target_date=YYYY-MM-DD
Returns: { posts: ScheduledPost[], count: int }
Fetches all scheduled_posts with status IN ('pending','generated')
for today in MSK timezone.
If count == 0 the workflow ends here (no-op).

nodes.name: Exit if no posts
nodes.notes: Branch: true → continue to generate; false → workflow ends.

nodes.name: Split posts into items
nodes.notes: Process each ScheduledPost individually so errors in one post do not block others.

nodes.name: Node 2 — Generate content
notes: Endpoint: POST /api/v1/content/{post_id}/generate
Body: { product_description: string }  (optional, enriches Claude prompt)
Returns: updated ScheduledPost with body filled and status='generated'
Skipped if post.status is already 'generated'.
The app uses ContentGenerator (Claude claude-sonnet-4-6) with token-bucket
rate limiting (0.5 req/s, burst=2).

nodes.name: Skip if already generated
nodes.notes: true → call generate endpoint; false → post already has body, proceed to publish.

nodes.name: Node 3 — Publish via Telegram publisher
notes: Endpoint: POST /api/v1/content/{post_id}/publish
Returns: { post_id, success: bool, telegram_message_id: int|null }
Internally calls publisher.publish_post() with 3-retry + exp backoff.
On success: CalendarManager.mark_published() records published_at + msg_id.
On failure: CalendarManager.mark_failed() + notify_publish_error() to admin.
Non-Telegram platforms (habr, vc.ru, linkedin) return success=true as stub.

nodes.name: Node 4 — Save metrics
notes: Endpoint: POST /api/v1/content/{post_id}/metrics
Currently a stub (returns {status: ok}); full implementation in Sprint 3
(Analytics Agent). Will collect Telegram views, reactions, forwards.

nodes.name: Node 5 — Telegram error alert to admin

parameter.name: text

notes: Fires on any unhandled error in nodes 1–4.
Sends alert directly to TELEGRAM_ADMIN_CHAT_ID via Bot API.
The app-side publisher also sends its own alert via notify_publish_error(),
so publish failures are double-reported for visibility.

meta.description: Content Agent pipeline: checks the editorial calendar 3x/day, generates post bodies via Claude API, publishes to Telegram channel, saves metrics.

endpoints.GET /api/v1/content/pending: Node 1 — fetch pending posts for today
endpoints.POST /api/v1/content/{id}/generate: Node 2 — fill post body via Claude API
endpoints.GET /api/v1/content/habr-draft/{id}/expo: On-demand: export Habr draft as Markdown
